name: 1-Wire Bus via GPIO
description: The `gpio` platform uses the CPU to generate the bus signals on an on-board GPIO pin.

config_entries.label: Pin
config_entries.description: The pin which will be use for bus communication. Note that 1-wire is a bi-directional bus so the selected GPIO pin must support both input and output. This must be a GPIO pin internal to the microcontroller and cannot be a pin located on an I/O expander or similar device.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID used for code generation. Required if you have multiple busses.
